headline: Boston Scientific Just Slashed Guidance and Wall Street Followed. Is the Pullback a Buying Opportunity?
summary: Boston Scientific (NYSE:BSX) stock has taken a beating in 2026 after management cut full-year guidance on Wednesday. On Thursday, three major firms trimmed price targets while keeping bullish ratings intact. The setup looks like a classic reset-the-bar, then-outperform scenario for patient investors. Wells Fargo lowered its price target on Boston Scientific stock to $75 from ... Boston Scientific Just Slashed Guidance and Wall Street Followed. Is the Pullback a Buying Opportunity?

headline: Why BSX Stock's Q1 Beat Is A Lagging Indicator
summary: The primary insight within Boston Scientific’s (BSX) Q1 2026 results is that the adjusted EPS beat of $0.80 acts as a lagging indicator, contrasting with a moderating trend in forward-looking growth. While management maintained a 28.0% operating margin, the decision to revise full-year 2026 organic revenue guidance from a 10%–11% range to 6.5%–8.0% is the critical signal for investors. This dynamic highlights a strategic divergence from other healthcare leaders. For instance, while our analysis

headline: BSX Q1 Deep Dive: Market Outperformance Despite Lowered Guidance and Product-Specific Headwinds
summary: Medical device company Boston Scientific (NYSE:BSX) announced better-than-expected revenue in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 11.6% year on year to $5.20 billion. On the other hand, next quarter’s revenue guidance of $5.39 billion was less impressive, coming in 2.9% below analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.80 per share was 1.6% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Is It Time To Reassess Boston Scientific (BSX) After Its Recent Share Price Slide

The stock recently closed at US$64.87, with returns of 0.4% over 7 days, a 6.7% decline over 30 days, a 31.5% decline year to date, and a 34.4% decline over 1 year, set against gains of 26.9% over 3 years and 46.6% over 5 years.
